title: Eva 2 for PSP info and screenshots
display_title: Eva 2 for PSP info and screenshots
wikitext:
[]: Eva 2 for PSP info and screenshots             Quote Post by Cogboy » Fri Feb 03, 2006 8:07 am Bandai has announced that they will be releasing a PSP game for GAINAXâ€™s animation â€œNeon Genesis Evangelionâ€. The game will be based on the â€œNeon Genesis Evangelion 2â€ released in November of 2003 on PS2, but with largely improved graphics, angels and battle, volume of the scenarios and better AI. source:[url]http://pspupdates.qj.net/2006/02/neon-genesis-evangelion-2-artificial.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Fri Feb 03, 2006 3:25 pm I'M GOING TO BUY PSP!!!!!! :shock: :shock: (:starts collecting money:) I hope it comes here (am I a bit ultraoptimistic? :P)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Feb 03, 2006 3:32 pm Six Winged Angel wrote: I hope it comes here (am I a bit ultraoptimistic? :P)

[]:             Quote Post by jonneh » Fri Feb 03, 2006 3:36 pm .............well fuck. that's just great. I just spent 90 years trying to decide between a DS and a PSP (i'm definitely not made of money), and I was looking at the games, and decided for DS, even though Katamari was coming for PSP. Now there's Katamari and freakin' Evangelion coming for PSP. - Is this game going to be just like the Eva 2 game for ps2, or just similar? - Any chances of a terribly-graphiced DS version? ;D haha - Translated into english? I wonder if we can have jap audio with eng subs.

[]:             Quote Post by Cogboy » Sat Feb 04, 2006 4:27 am It seems to be a largely imroved version of the original for ps2, even with full control of the eva's in battle. definately an import title for me :)

[]:             Quote Post by Cogboy » Sun Feb 05, 2006 6:57 pm two new famitsu scans. page 1 page 2 warning, high res images. source: [url]http://gamersreports.com/news/1228/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Mon Feb 06, 2006 11:03 am i got a PSP the first time i tried - in the UK - everyone panicing about shortages; just go to small games shops and they have plenty. mind, it is a Japanese one - which is actually better - high def screen...... which is pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Mon Feb 06, 2006 1:55 pm I'd like to see that... heh... My only problem with these is that I just can't find any English ones around my place... But I want to play with that Girlfriend of Steel!!! :roll: :oops: :evil:

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Tue Feb 07, 2006 12:54 pm D: Now I HAVE to buy a PSP with my tax returns.

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Wed Feb 08, 2006 7:16 pm I'm sold. When is this game comeing to the U.S.?

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Thu Feb 09, 2006 5:11 pm BLACKANGEL32076 wrote: I'm sold. When is this game comeing to the U.S.?

[]:             Quote Post by Cogboy » Fri Feb 10, 2006 6:50 am More news: Bandai has announced the release information of â€œNeon Genesis Evangelion 2: Artificial World - Another casesâ€. This is the completely new â€œEVA2â€. The quality and volume will be very satisfying on the PSP. All the elements have been enhanced, and will be delivered in the retail version. â€“ Game Points * Free And Real Game World Overwhelming scenario script contains up to 37,000 words! Original game events also largely extended. Newly recorded voice added. The player will act as one of the characters of â€œEVAâ€, and experience their daily life. You will be able to enjoy the advanced AI and surreal life/chatting in the game. * Colorful High Definition Graphics High quality and volume designed especially for the PSP. The wide-screen of the PSP will be offering powerful screen effects to the players. With the high performance of the PSP, players will be able to experience pretty game and event graphics. The 3D â€œEVAâ€ world will be offering the feelings of reality. New maps also added. * New Threats, Powerful And Reality Battles New element â€œAssist Actionâ€ added. Players will be fighting the angles with the help of the associates. The AI has been improved for both enemies and friends. New actions and weapons will add more weight to the strategy part. Two versions of packages will be available when the game is release: * Neon Genesis Evangelion 2: Artificial World - Another cases Normal Version. Price 5,040 JPY ($43 USD) [Order] * Neon Genesis Evangelion 2: Artificial World - Another cases Periodical Limited Version. Price 10,500 JPY ($89.7 USD) [Order] Added with 2 UMD Video containing the OVA version â€œThe End of Evangelionâ€. Both versions will be released on April 27 in Japan. Source:[url]http://pspupdates.qj.net/2006/02/neon-genesis-evangelion-2-_113955757450178493.html[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by jonneh » Fri Feb 10, 2006 8:57 am That new information sounds really nice. Unfortunately, with me not understanding a lick of Japanese character's (just a few words here and there), I don't think I'll get this game unless a translation comes out someday (which hopefully will). I know it's not likely, I'm just getting my hopes up anyway, heh. One of the reasons for this is because you get to chat with people and experience the alternate universe and everything, it's not just battling. So I'd like to be able to actually understand it, instead of saving and trial-and-error, like I did with " Girlfriend of Steel 2 ". :P

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Feb 10, 2006 9:13 am If it did come out in the US, I'd buy a PSP just to play it. The only reason I bought a PS2 was to play FFX.

[]:             Quote Post by phanthompain » Fri Feb 10, 2006 10:33 am Well I am still stuck with he N64 eva game, man I really need a PSP. :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Feb 10, 2006 11:29 am To all PSP owners: The PSP does not use region encoding for its games. So if you want to import Evangelion 2, you will be able to play it on your domestic PSP. Whether you know what the hell you're doing is your problem.

[]:             Quote Post by max payne » Sun Feb 12, 2006 4:17 pm did the game on the ps2 version come out in america or england? The N64 version was terrible, it was just a bundle of mini games tied together with a thin string :(

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Feb 12, 2006 4:47 pm max payne wrote: did the game on the ps2 version come out in america or england?

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Sun Feb 12, 2006 6:28 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: To all PSP owners: The PSP does not use region encoding for its games. So if you want to import Evangelion 2, you will be able to play it on your domestic PSP. Whether you know what the hell you're doing is your problem.

[]:             Quote Post by Cogboy » Sun Feb 12, 2006 7:03 pm More scans guys! [url]http://pspupdates.qj.net/2006/02/neon-genesis-evangelion-2-artificial_12.html[/url]
